title: Fractionally differentiated log-price feature (AFML Ch5, FFD)
source: LdP literature mining (2 parallel read-only agents over AFML Ch5/17/18/19 + HFT, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/extracted), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'hay alguna otra nueva feature que se puede extraer de la literatura'.
why: Every one of the 23 current tier2 features is integer-differenced (d=1: r_1..r_20, z_r20, macd, rsi) and therefore memory-less past a finite window. FFD (fixed-width window, Snippet 5.3) injects the LONG-MEMORY price-level information that all of them structurally destroy, while staying stationary (book: corr 0.995 to level at d~=0.35 vs 0.03 for returns). This is the single most direct attack on the documented orthogonal-feature bottleneck (MEMORY hypothesizer_persona_ceiling) and the highest information-gain-per-risk feature found in the whole literature sweep.
value_rationale: STRONG-ADOPT (build first). Cheap FIR filter, causal, book-validated on daily commodity-like data; genuinely orthogonal to the entire existing differenced/momentum/vol block.
scope: Implement getWeights_FFD + fracDiff_FFD (Snippets 5.1/5.3) on log(close); add FFD feature to build_technical_features
Pick d either hard-coded (~0.4, per Table 5.1 commodities) OR min-d-passing-ADF on the TRAIN slice of each WF fold only — never full-sample (lookahead)
Use fixed-width FFD, NOT expanding-window fracDiff (avoids negative-drift artifact, Snippet 5.2)
Validate causality: weights are history-only; add a test that the feature at t uses only bars <= t
recommendation: Adopt; the one feature that fills the long-memory gap. Start with fixed d~=0.4 to avoid any train-time tuning appearance.

external_refs.label: AFML Ch5 §5.4-5.6 Snippets 5.1-5.4 (getWeights_FFD, fracDiff_FFD, min-d ADF)

history.reason: LdP literature mining: frac-diff log-price is the top orthogonal-driver candidate
feat(features): B0134 fractionally-differentiated log-price (FFD, AFML Ch5) + expert-guided backlog

Already implemented on 2026-06-03 (commit 'feat(features): B0134 fractionally-differentiated log-price (FFD, AFML Ch5)') but the entry was never closed - status hygiene close, verified 2026-06-11: pipeline/frac_features.py frac_diff_ffd (fixed-width FFD, Snippet 5.3), ffd_logclose wired into BOTH D1 and H4 technical builders with hard-coded d=0.3 / thres=1e-3 (no train-time tuning, no full-sample ADF lookahead), 25 frac/ffd tests green incl. causality (prefix-stability) test. Scope items all satisfied; dossiers will carry the feature on next rebuild.
